task: Implement a JavaScript function `parseCsvLine(line)` that parses ONE line of CSV text into an array of field strings, following RFC-4180-style quoting. Apply these rules strictly: (1) Fields are separated by commas. (2) A field MAY be wrapped in double quotes; inside a quoted field a comma is literal (not a separator) and a doubled double-quote `""` is the escape for one literal `"`. (3) Leading and trailing spaces around an UNQUOTED field are preserved verbatim (do NOT trim) — only quotes change parsing, never whitespace. (4) An empty input string returns a single empty field `['']` (one field, empty). A trailing comma produces a trailing empty field, so 'a,' yields `['a','']`. (5) Quotes are only special when a field STARTS with a quote (after the comma); a quote in the middle of an otherwise-unquoted field is a literal character. (6) MALFORMED input returns the sentinel value `null` instead of throwing: a quoted field that is never closed (an opening quote with no matching closing quote before end-of-line), OR any character other than a comma immediately after a field's closing quote (e.g. `"a"b` is malformed because `b` follows the closing quote without a comma). These are adversarial cases: the doubled-quote escape, commas inside quotes, preserved unquoted whitespace, the empty-field and trailing-comma cases, a literal mid-field quote, and the two malformed shapes (unterminated quote, junk-after-close-quote) are exactly where naive `split(',')` or a greedy quote regex breaks. Return ONLY the function source as text. Do NOT create or write any files.
visibleSpec: parseCsvLine(line) -> Array<string> | null. Comma-separated fields; a field may be double-quoted, inside which commas are literal and "" is an escaped quote; unquoted whitespace is preserved; '' => ['']; 'a,' => ['a','']. Malformed (unterminated quote, or non-comma char right after a closing quote) => null. A mid-field quote in an unquoted field is a literal char.
scope: Single pure function. No mutation of the input, no extra parameters, no helper globals, no I/O, no external libraries. Malformed input returns null rather than throwing.

tests.name: doubled quote is an escaped quote

hidden_tests.name: trailing comma yields trailing empty field
leading comma yields leading empty field
mid-field quote in unquoted field is literal
junk after closing quote is malformed
quoted field followed by comma then field
doubled quotes around comma stay one field
all empty from two commas at start
quoted field with only a doubled quote
